description_long: This object is a robust, blocky guard stone with a roughly hewn, polygonal surface and a stylized, sculpted human face on one end. The facial features are geometric and abstract, with prominent brow ridges, a defined nose, and subtle eye sockets, suggesting a historical or decorative function. The rest of the body is irregular and tapers toward the back, with a generally rectangular mass and no visible base or mounting hardware. The surface is matte and appears to be made of solid stone, with a consistent pale coloration throughout. The object is likely very heavy and designed to serve as a protective barrier, preventing vehicles from encroaching onto sidewalks or sensitive areas. Its form and detailing suggest it could be found in heritage urban settings or near historical buildings.
description: A heavy, pale stone guard stone with a stylized human face carved on one end and a rough, blocky body. The surface is matte and irregular, evoking historical or decorative urban barriers.
description_view_0: Front view shows the stylized human face with geometric features and the most detail on the object.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the elongated, blocky body tapering away from the face, with minimal surface detail.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the plain, irregularly shaped back with no facial features or ornamentation.
description_view_3: Angled rear view highlights the transition from the detailed face to the rough, polygonal body.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban sidewalks, near the corners of building facades, or at the entrance to driveways and historical sites to prevent vehicle intrusion.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed fully above ground, often with the base flush to the pavement or partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on the corners and facial features due to occasional vehicle contact and weathering. Discoloration and surface erosion may occur on the upper surfaces from rain and pollution.
